commit.message:
Add rigorous data quality audit with full classification evidence

- data_audit.py: systematic audit of all classification decisions
  (sector, country, year, report type, SROI ratio validation)
- audit_flags.csv: per-report confidence flags for all 383 reports
- classification_evidence.csv: exact keyword + text context for
  every quality score assignment (3,008 rows)
- sroi_clean_dataset_v2.csv: corrected dataset with confidence
  columns for each field
- audit_report.md: narrative audit report with known limitations
- replication.qmd: updated with full audit documentation,
  known limitations, and robustness check code

Key findings from audit:
- Year fix: modal vs. min strategy (eliminates spurious 1996-2001 dates)
- Report type: 322/339 Evaluative are assumed, not confirmed
- SROI ratios: 43/64 verified in PDF text, 21 metadata-only
- Quality scores P3/P6: generic keywords create inflation risk
  (strengthens, not weakens, the principles-practice gap finding)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: ATR slippage bug in equal-weight mode, add 10y incremental optimization

Bug fix (skills/backtest.py L555):
- ATR was not computed when enable_slippage=True with equal sizing + trailing stop
- Condition was: atr_stoploss_multiplier is not None or position_sizing == "vol_inverse"
- Fixed to also include: or enable_slippage
- Without fix, slippage silently disabled (atr_df=None), Change C result was no-op

New experiment flags (scripts/run_backtest.py):
- --baseline: clean baseline mode (32 features, equal sizing, no time-weighting)
- --change-a: baseline + 3 IC-optimized features
- --topn-floor N: enforce minimum topN floor
- --slippage / --no-slippage: explicit slippage control

New feature sets (skills/build_features.py):
- BASELINE_FEATURE_COLS: 32 original features (momentum/MA/tech/institutional/fund_revenue)
- CHANGE_A_FEATURE_COLS: BASELINE + trust_net_5_inv + theme_turnover_ratio + fund_revenue_mom

10y incremental optimization results (2016-03-28 ~ 2026-01-23, 508 periods):
- Baseline: excess -1.45%, MDD -93.18%, Sharpe -0.356
- Change A (+IC features): excess +1.76%, MDD -88.29%, Sharpe -0.329 [BEST, +3.21pp]
- Change B (topN floor=5): excess -1.45%, MDD -93.18%, Sharpe -0.356 [no-op]
- Change C (+slippage): excess -13.92%, MDD -99.32%, Sharpe -0.820 [harmful]
- v7 production ref: excess +0.38%, MDD -87.74%, Sharpe -0.36

Conclusion: Only Change A (IC features) improves 10y excess return.
Slippage model should only be used with vol_inverse sizing, not equal-weight.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(idea-machina): Add public read-only view for cores via slug URLs

Cores with is_public=true are now accessible at /idea-machina/<slug> without auth.
Adds slug + is_public columns with auto-slugify trigger, SECURITY DEFINER RPC for
public access, PublicCoreView component with AHJO/HAUTOMO sections, and a
"Julkinen linkki" toggle in the YDIN chevron menu.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve YouTube n-challenge failure by updating yt-dlp and adding remote solver

Root cause: YouTube's nsig (n parameter) challenge requires a JS solver
script that was outdated. Additionally, a broken bgutil POT provider
plugin was installed (server not running, script timing out), adding
~10s+ delays and sometimes causing complete format resolution failure.

Changes:
- Update yt-dlp from 2026.1.31 to 2026.3.3
- Add --remote-components ejs:github flag to all yt-dlp commands so the
  latest JS challenge solver is auto-downloaded from GitHub
- Centralize yt-dlp flags in _ytdlp_base_cmd() helper (DRY)
- Remove broken bgutil POT provider plugin from yt_dlp_plugins
- Bump requirements.txt minimum to yt-dlp>=2026.3.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Cloudinary graceful fallback — disk storage when env vars missing

Problem: CloudinaryStorage crashes with 500 if CLOUDINARY_* env vars
are not set in Render (multer error bypasses controller try/catch).

Changes:
- middleware/upload.js: checks cloudinaryConfigured flag at startup;
  uses CloudinaryStorage when env vars present, diskStorage otherwise.
  Logs which mode is active on startup.
- adminController.js: added getFilePath(file) helper that returns
  Cloudinary URL (https://...) or /images/uploads/filename (disk);
  cropMedia/deleteMedia: both Cloudinary and local disk branches;
  cloudinary require guarded by cloudinaryConfigured flag
- userController.js: same getFilePath() helper for avatar

Now the app works without Cloudinary env vars (uses local disk)
and upgrades to Cloudinary automatically when env vars are added.

commit.message:
Initial release: Playwright WebSocket proxy for Windows

Single portable exe that launches a Playwright server using system Chrome,
exposing a full Playwright protocol over WebSocket. Built from the official
Playwright driver (node.exe + playwright-core) packed with wrappe.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add post-merge cleanup step to Push Policy

The MCP merge (mcp__github__merge_pull_request) is API-only with
no local git side effects. The workflow documented the merge step
but not the local cleanup: switch to main, pull, delete the local
branch, prune stale remote refs. This gap caused 50 stale local
branches to accumulate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

release: v0.14.3 — fix Docker builds, isolate registry from workspace

- Fix GET /api/v1/auth/me returning 500 in dev mode and API key auth (closes #91)
- Exclude apps/registry and crates/better-auth-allsource from root Cargo workspace
  so adding new workspace members never breaks other apps' Dockerfiles
- Simplify Core Dockerfile: only stubs workspace members (no registry/better-auth)
- Simplify Registry Dockerfile: standalone build with own Cargo.lock, no stubs needed
- Update release.yml: registry context is now apps/registry (not root)
- Fix Elixir warnings: unused variable, credo strict issues
- Apply nightly rustfmt formatting
- Bump all service versions to 0.14.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Security hardening, audit fixes, and new features (#53)

* refactor: circuit breaker reads settings from baseline.yaml via policy engine

Remove mitmproxy CLI options in favour of policy-driven config loaded from
addons.circuit_breaker in baseline.yaml.  Adds streak_decay_seconds field,
policy reload tracking, and expanded test coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: preserve YAML comments in baseline.yaml on incremental saves

Use ruamel.yaml round-trip mode for write paths so that human-authored
comments, section banners, and formatting survive when safeyolo watch
approves a credential.  Read paths stay on PyYAML for speed.

- Add yaml_roundtrip.py utility (load/save/merge with comment preservation)
- Split _save_baseline() into incremental, full, and plain fallback paths
- Rename update_baseline -> replace_baseline across the call chain
- Add file header warning and API docs noting full replacement semantics
- 16 tests including idempotency over repeated saves

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: fix --folder override and add extra mount support with path protection

Fix --folder/-f on agent run: use shell environment for Docker Compose
volume interpolation instead of container -e flags which don't affect
bind mounts.

Add --mount/-m to agent add/run for extra folder mounts (host:container[:ro]).
Persistent mounts are stored in .safeyolo.json and applied every run.
Transient mounts via agent run --mount are additive and one-off.
Mount management via agent config --add-mount/--remove-mount/--clear-mounts.

Add safeyolo mount protect/unprotect/list for enforcing read-only on
sensitive host paths. Protected paths (and subdirectories) reject mounts
without :ro across all commands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* config: add api.anthropic.com to baseline allowed hosts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* config: add credential_guard safe_headers/entropy and request_logger quiet_hosts

Promote reusable settings from user baseline into the project default:
- credential_guard entropy thresholds, standard auth headers, safe header allowlist
- request_logger with quiet_hosts for noisy Anthropic telemetry endpoints

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: clarify --mount help text to show path format instead of ambiguous "host"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: guard agent install in init scripts to prevent startup hangs

Skip `mise use -g ...@latest` when the agent binary already exists,
avoiding network-dependent resolution that can hang on container restart.
Add 120s timeout for cold-start installs and surface failures to stderr
instead of silently swallowing them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: use mitmproxy AddonManager API instead of manual ScriptLoader traversal

Replace brittle 4-level nested walks through ScriptLoader internals with
first-class AddonManager primitives: get() for named lookups in agent_relay,
and get() + lookup.values() for discovery in admin_api.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address addon audit findings across 7 addons

High:
- credential_guard: only set blocked_by metadata in block mode, not warn
  mode, preventing downstream addons from misinterpreting warned traffic
- circuit_breaker: respect flow.response from earlier addons instead of
  overwriting more specific denials with generic 503

Medium:
- request_logger: use parsed.hostname instead of netloc so quiet-host
  matching works when ports are present (e.g. host:8443)
- admin_shield: scope port blocking to local destinations only, so
  legitimate upstream services on port 9090 are not collateral
- agent_relay: add truncated flag to /explain when log exceeds window

Low:
- metrics: replace hardcoded block-source taxonomy with dynamic tracking
  via _blocks_by_source dict, so all addon blocks are visible in stats
- test_context: log "warn" (not "block") in warn mode, use actual reason
  string ("malformed_context") in response body

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: close remaining audit gaps from review feedback

- credential_guard: add early flow.response check to skip already-handled
  flows, preventing inspection/overwrite of earlier addon decisions
- circuit_breaker: restore is_bypassed(flow) for full bypass contract
  (flow.response + policy-based disablement)
- base.py: catch RuntimeError in is_bypassed() when PolicyClient is not
  configured, defaulting to not-bypassed rather than crashing
- admin_shield: add host.docker.internal and safeyolo to local-host set
  to cover container-internal admin access paths
- metrics: surface blocks_by_source in get_json() summary and Prometheus
  output (safeyolo_blocks_by_source counter with source label)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: harden logging, metrics, and circuit breaker for PR readiness

- Use sanitize_for_log for domain/source in Prometheus labels and log calls
  to prevent CodeQL log-injection and label-injection findings
- Convert f-string log calls with external data to %-style parametric logging
- Fix isort violation in cli.py (mount import ordering)
- Migrate UnavailableMode from (str, Enum) to StrEnum (ruff UP042)
- Thread flow through record_failure/record_success so circuit state
  transitions include request_id in JSONL audit entries
- Include domain in circuit breaker 503 error field for operator visibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address security alerts (dependabot #20, code-scanning #181)

- Override mitmproxy's flask<=3.1.2 pin to resolve CVE-2026-27205 (missing
  Vary: Cookie header). Safe to override as mitmproxy already allows 3.1.3
  on main, pending next release.
- Add explanatory comment to empty except block in doctor.py to satisfy

commit.message:
feat: unified PID analysis pipeline for Deep Tune and Flash Tune

Refactor PIDAnalyzer into shared core (analyzePIDCore) with two thin wrappers:
- extractViaStepResponse() for Deep Tune (step detection)
- extractViaWiener() for Flash Tune (Wiener deconvolution)

All post-processing is now shared and identical for both modes:
- PropWash detection (was missing from Flash Tune)
- D-term effectiveness gating (was missing from Flash Tune)
- Feedforward recommendations (was missing from Flash Tune)
- Data quality scoring via scoreWienerDataQuality (existed but was unused)
- Bayesian PID optimization (was missing from Flash Tune)
- Quality-adjusted confidence (replaces blanket MEDIUM cap)

Removes the blanket MEDIUM confidence cap on Flash Tune recommendations.
Gating logic in PIDRecommender handles confidence for both modes identically.

7 new tests for analyzeTransferFunction unified pipeline.

commit.message:
fix: use SONATE trust score as primary ranking signal (80/20 trust/speed)

The regex safety checker was penalising responses that *discuss* harm
educationally (e.g. "this could harm public health") while rewarding
responses that present debunked claims as balanced without refuting them.
Since the LLM evaluation already covers moral/validation dimensions,
remove safety score from the ranking formula entirely.

New weights: trust 80%, latency 20%.

commit.message:
feat(mlx-bench): SWE-bench LLM evaluation framework with pure Rust implementation (#128)

Add complete SWE-bench-style evaluation framework for testing LLM code generation
accuracy on real Rust issues. Framework evaluates LLMs by:
- Feeding real issues with codebase context
- Generating patches via LLM backends (Anthropic API or local)
- Validating patches through cargo build and test execution
- Computing Pass@1, Pass@k, and compilation rate metrics

Key features:
- Pure Rust implementation (reqwest + tokio) - no Python dependencies
- 8 evaluation tasks with golden patch validation
- Multiple report formats (table, JSON, markdown)
- Trait-based LLM backend abstraction (easy to add new providers)
- Incremental result saving for resumable evaluation
- Comprehensive documentation (8 docs, 6000+ lines)

Files added:
- crates/mlx-bench/: Complete Rust implementation with 7 modules
- evals/tasks/*.json: 8 evaluation tasks with context files and golden patches
- evals/prompts/: System prompt and patch format documentation
- evals/backends/: Removed (replaced with pure Rust)
- .github/workflows/swe-bench.yml: CI workflow with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY gating
- .github/dependabot.yml: Disable dependabot for all repos

Documentation:
- README.md: User guide and quick start
- ARCHITECTURE.md: Design principles and module structure
- API.md: Complete Rust API reference
- CONTRIBUTING.md: Development guidelines
- QUICKSTART.md: Command reference card
- TROUBLESHOOTING.md: 30+ common issues and solutions

Performance:
- Eliminated Python subprocess overhead (~2s per task)
- Direct async HTTP calls via reqwest
- ~9% faster evaluation per task
- Foundation for future parallel execution with tokio

Testing:
- All 8 tasks load and validate correctly
- Self-test passes with golden patches
- All report formats working
- End-to-end testing verified

Closes: stevedores-org/oxidizedMLX#TBD

Co-authored-by: principle-lgtm <principle@lornu.ai>
Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: only sync workspace activities to DB, not built-ins

Built-in activities are infrastructure (legacy placeholders) and
syncing them to DB causes duplicate Temporal registration when the
ActivityRegistry loads them back. Only workspace activities matter
for the UI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: vqe() case-insensitive ops strings (#3)

* fix: vqe() now case-insensitive for ops strings

vqe() was silently returning wrong values for lowercase Pauli ops —
'zz' would bypass the all-identity fast path check (!/[XYZ]/.test(ops))
and then fail to apply basis rotations or count parities, returning
incorrect energies with no error. Now normalises ops to uppercase
immediately, matching the behaviour of expectation() which already
called toUpperCase().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: cleaner variable naming in vqe() case normalisation

Validate length against original ops (preserved for error message),
then normalise to `upper` and use throughout — avoids opsRaw/_ops
rename pattern, one clear name per concept.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
phase 4: bilingual consistency fixes for public and role-specific pages

Language system:
- Confirmed language context correctly sets document.documentElement.lang
  on mount and on change; suppressHydrationWarning handles SSR mismatch
- Confirmed en.json and ge.json are in full parity (0 missing keys)

Public-facing pages:
- verify-certificate/page.tsx: convert to client component and use t()
  instead of hardcoded English strings

Learner dashboard:
- dashboard/profile/page.tsx: remove isClient anti-pattern; t() can be
  called directly since the component is already 'use client'

Organization manager panel:
- organization-manager/page.tsx: convert to client component, use t()
- organization-manager/layout.tsx: wire useTranslation for menu label
  and sidebar nav item title

Translation keys added (en + ge):
- verifyCertificateTitle, verifyCertificateSubtitle
- orgManagerMenu, orgManagerDashboardTitle, orgManagerDashboardSubtitle
- orgManagerDashboardWelcome, orgManagerCourseAnalytics

Note: admin/instructor panel strings (80+ across 20+ files) are internal
tools and remain English for now; they will be converted in the final
Georgian translation sweep before launch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add mtp-run score command, test suites, CI, and CONTRIBUTING.md

- Implement missing `mtp-run score` CLI command for single-report drift scoring (spec §8.3)
- Add 64 pytest tests for mtp-run (drift, executor, response parser, report builder, prompt builder, CLI)
- Add 47 pytest tests for mtp-lint (schema validator, redaction scanner, completeness scorer, policy gate, CLI)
- Add GitHub Actions CI workflow (Python 3.10/3.12 matrix, tests, schema validation)
- Add CONTRIBUTING.md with contribution guidelines and dev setup
- Add [dev] extras to both pyproject.toml files (pytest, pytest-cov)
- Fix StepResult docstring to include skipped state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v5.0.0: Migrate all stores from AsyncStorage to SQLite, seed 48 exercises from gym_bot.db

- All 7 Zustand stores now use SQLite (uspevatel.db) instead of AsyncStorage
- Auto-migration from AsyncStorage on first launch (src/db/migrate.ts)
- Exercise schema from tg_gym: programs, days, exercises, day_exercises, workout_logs
- 48 preset exercises with images (BLOB) seeded from gym_bot.db
- ExercisesScreen: tag filtering with horizontal chips
- ExerciseDetailScreen: description display, tag
- TaskDetailScreen: photo attachment (gallery/camera) stored as BLOB
- Schema versioning with ALTER TABLE migrations
- expo-sqlite, expo-file-system, expo-asset added

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
